title: meal-prep dense slovakian winey gratin
description: a hearty, comforting dish made with buckwheat and served in a cedar plank

ingredients.name: grated carrot
ingredients.notes: use a box grater or food processor to get the right texture

ingredients.name: grated parsnip
ingredients.notes: use a box grater or food processor to get the right texture

ingredients.name: dried cranberry
ingredients.notes: soak in wine for at least 30 minutes before adding to dish

ingredients.name: unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: use a mixture of browned and plain butter for added depth of flavor

ingredients.name: dry white wine
ingredients.notes: use a wine that is low in tannins and won't overpower the other flavors

ingredients.name: grated ginger
ingredients.notes: use fresh ginger for the best flavor

ingredients.name: minced garlic
ingredients.notes: use 2-3 cloves if you like a stronger garlic flavor

ingredients.name: plain Greek yogurt
ingredients.notes: adds a tangy, creamy element to the dish

instructions: in a medium saucepan, bring 2 cups of water to a boil. add buckwheat groats and reduce heat to low. cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the water is absorbed and the groats are tender.
while the buckwheat is cooking, soak the dried cranberry in wine for at least 30 minutes. this will help to soften them and add flavor to the dish.
in a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. add grated carrot and parsnip and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are tender and lightly browned, about 10-12 minutes.
add grated ginger and minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
stir in the soaked cranberry and wine, orange zest, and plain Greek yogurt. cook for 1-2 minutes, until the mixture is hot and bubbly.
in a separate skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. add the cooked buckwheat and stir until it is well coated with the butter.
transfer the buckwheat mixture to a cedar plank or a 9x13 inch baking dish. top with the carrot-parsnip mixture and dot the top with the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter.
bake in preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned and the dish is hot and bubbly.
remove from oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
serve hot, garnished with additional orange zest if desired.
store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
